block_01: Netflix released its Q1 Earnings earlier this week, causing the stock to take a fall after hours.
block_02: The streaming giant reported a total of 139 million paying subscribers at the end of 2018. This figure, although higher than the subscriber count of its current competitors, Amazon Prime, & Hulu, was only expected to increase by 5 million in Q2; this is considered weak, as it is 8% less growth, compared to this time last year.
block_03: In addition, subscribers in the US, Brazil, Mexico & some parts of Europe, will likely see an increase in their membership fees.
block_04: This news, along with the expected release of other streaming services later this year, & next; including Disney, WarnerMedia, & Apple, will undoubtedly cause investors to consider their investment choices in the future.
block_05: Netflix has expressed it is "excited to compete" with its new rivals, adding: "we don't anticipate that these new entrants will materially affect our growth."
block_06: Nevertheless, the world is watching closely, as are we; to trade or to “chill”?
